skills: paraphrase-matching of a personal-interest passage (precise wording matters, not just topical overlap)
cultural food-picture classification (identify "American food" among several cuisines)
environmental sign meaning (Think Green / Do Not Litter)
fun-fact paraphrase with numeric-entity confusion distractor (five hearts vs five earthworms)
precise traffic/road-sign discrimination (left turn / right turn / no-left / no-right)
multi-turn map/direction reading with named streets
dialogue pragmatic-inference (deriving an implied fact not directly stated, e.g. "will have to wait until 3pm")
advanced punctuation correctness (question mark vs semicolon vs exclamation nuance)
dialogue completion across many everyday settings (18 items, items 15-32)
grammar: present continuous vs simple present for facts
polite request forms (Could you please help me)
punctuation: question marks vs semicolons vs exclamation marks
question_types: short 2-line dialogue completion across many settings (18 items)
reading_patterns: short personal-interest statement ("My hobbies are...") that must be restated with exact topical fidelity - a partially-overlapping distractor (mentions only one of two stated hobbies) is wrong
dialogue_patterns: polite-request-for-help exchange requiring correct modal register
opinion-about-a-person exchange (personality trait, not physical description)
privacy-refusal exchange (won't give phone number to a stranger)
emotional-valence-congruence exchange (looks sad -> reveals a negative reason)
caution/warning-response exchange (heavy object -> logical safety action)
interrupted-task exchange (mother calling -> resuming an order request)
surprise-and-correction exchange (mistaken assumption about a person corrected)
occasion-guessing exchange from a family-photo picture with an extra distinguishing clue
distractor_patterns: partially-overlapping paraphrase (restates only one of two stated facts)
numeric-entity confusion (confusing the count of hearts with the count of animals)
tense used inconsistent with the stated time reference
wrong punctuation mark for the sentence's grammatical type
topically related but logically unsupported inference
answer_logic: paraphrase-matching items require exact fidelity to all stated facts, not just partial topical overlap
pragmatic-inference dialogue items require deriving an implied consequence from stated facts (e.g. box office hours) rather than a directly stated answer
sign-discrimination items test precise visual/semantic discrimination between near-identical icons
occasion-guessing items require using an explicitly stated distinguishing clue to override an initial plausible guess
notes: Exam sat 10 Feb 2567 (BE+1 sitting-date convention). 32 items; items 1-14 standalone/reading, items 15-32 dialogue. Punctuation and pragmatic-inference items continue to increase in sophistication relative to 2562-2563.
